items.title: Deferred-loading ergonomics

items.title: Edit-retry root causes
items.summary: The packet reports "31 failed-edit-then-retry sequences" but does not separate prompt quality, workflow design, or messaging causes. The repo can improve guardrails now, but root-cause attribution remains open.

items.title: Plugin-overhead net costing
items.summary: What is the actual gross-savings-minus-remedy-overhead result for /clear plus plugin-memory recovery once injected context, hook writes, and skill surfaces are counted?

items.title: Baseline-window definition for skill review
items.summary: What minimum observation window, routing denominator, and error bucket are required before a skill can honestly be labeled never_used or low_frequency?

items.summary: GORM is materially looser: a struct counts as a model if its body contains gorm.Model, gorm.DeletedAt, a gorm: tag, or even a plain json: tag, which over-admits ordinary DTO structs. Mislabels as "ast". (external/src/ast

items.title: Monorepo aggregation only understands pnpm-workspace.yaml and package.json workspaces
items.summary: Monorepo aggregation only understands pnpm-workspace.yaml and package.json workspaces. There are no dedicated branches for turbo.json, nx.json, lerna.json, or Rush manifests. .turbo appears only as an ignored directory.

items.summary: disableDetectors is a true short-circuit before invocation, not a post-filter. Disabled built-ins return resolved empty placeholders; plugin detectors still run. (external/src/index.ts:99-130)

items.title: CodesightPlugin is a real typed contract (name, optional async detector, optional async postProcessor)
items.summary: CodesightPlugin is a real typed contract (name, optional async detector, optional async postProcessor). Plugin detectors merge routes/schemas/components/middleware into the in-memory result; post-processors run after sca

items.title: Component extraction is shallow and React-biased

items.title: Telemetry is local-only and opt-in
items.summary: Telemetry is local-only and opt-in. --telemetry imports runTelemetry, reads project files + the generated CODESIGHT.md, computes the same Math.ceil(text.length / 4) token estimate, and writes a local telemetry.md report.

items.title: Config-gated bootstrap layering
items.summary: Contextador's bootstrap split is still one of the cleanest ideas in the repo: local operation first, shared-cache/provider behavior only when configured. Public can adopt that layering around existing retrieval substrate

items.title: Generated .mcp.json project scaffold plus setup hints
items.summary: The actual behavior here is modest but useful: the wizard captures setup intent, init creates the project MCP file, and doctor tells the user when that file is missing. Public already has richer runtime recovery after in

items.title: Benchmark-honest token reporting
items.summary: Contextador's failure mode is instructive: the implementation says est. while the README markets a stronger percentage than the code proves. Public should keep the implementation honesty and extend it into docs and repor

items.title: Model-first routing facade over existing Public tools
items.summary: Contextador's routing split is useful because it cleanly separates model-first scope selection from deterministic fallback. Public could expose a similar facade that decides when to consult semantic search, memory, or gr

items.title: Query-triggered repair and deterministic enrichment
items.summary: The feedback-to-repair loop remains the strongest additive behavior in Contextador. Public could experiment with a narrow version that patches stale research or context artifacts only after verified misses, with full aud

items.title: Janitor-style staged freshness pass
items.summary: The staged Janitor plus freshness heuristics still suggest a good maintenance shape for artifact systems. Public already has readiness and freshness checks, but not a comparable staged repair sweep for context artifacts.

items.title: Mainframe-style shared answer cache with local budget gating
items.summary: The interesting part of Mainframe is not generic collaboration; it is replayable answer reuse keyed by recent query history, plus local guardrails around expensive shared behavior. Public does not currently expose that a

items.title: Bounded event-driven automation pipeline
items.summary: The GitHub layer shows a reusable pattern: intake, deterministic narrowing, optional model adjudication, targeted remediation, and append-only event logging. Public could reuse that pattern for selected automation flows

items.title: Unified provider and setup abstraction
items.summary: Contextador's provider surface and setup wizard are not retrieval breakthroughs, but they do reduce cognitive load. Public could benefit from a single place that explains and validates which providers or host modes are a

items.title: Pointer-only context delivery as the primary response model
items.summary: Contextador's served payload is deliberately small, but it drops too much structure and narrative detail. Public already has richer content-plus-structure surfaces, so replacing them with a pointer-only markdown outline

items.title: Contextador as a semantic or structural retrieval replacement
items.summary: The traced source shows no vector index on the live path and no symbol-level structural traversal. Public already covers those domains with CocoIndex and Code Graph MCP, so adopting Contextador's retrieval substrate woul

items.title: Mainframe privacy and conflict model as-is
items.summary: Persistent local identities, plaintext local secrets, broad room-history metadata, blind shared-state writes, and shallow conflict handling are too weak for direct adoption. The collaboration concept is interesting, but

items.title: Bun runtime coupling
items.summary: Contextador's CLI and setup flows are Bun-native. Public should treat that as an implementation detail of the external repo, not as a reason to reshape its own runtime stack. Existing Public surface is sufficient because

items.title: Direct source adoption under AGPL/commercial licensing
items.summary: The licensing model alone makes direct source reuse the wrong default. Even where the ideas are attractive, the safe path is to reimplement the pattern rather than import the code. Existing Public surface is sufficient a

items.title: The 71.5x token-reduction claim is mathematically reproducible
items.summary: (123,488 / 1,726 = 71.55x) but rests on three load-bearing assumptions: (a) naive baseline = full-corpus stuffing, (b) 4-chars-per-token heuristic, (c) BFS subgraph approximates real query cost. The 8.8x code-only number

items.title: The pipeline is a 7-stage chain detect → extract → build → cluster → analyze → report → export
items.summary: with extract internally split into parallel structural (AST tree-sitter) and semantic (Claude subagent) branches. The split is implemented as inline shell-invoked Python in skill.md, not as a unified Python function. AST

items.title: Cache invalidation is a TWO-LAYER stack
items.summary: (manifest mtime + SHA256 content hash), not a single mechanism. Layer 1 filters which files to consider, Layer 2 decides whether to actually re-extract. The two layers protect different failure modes (file edited vs file

items.title: The AST pass dispatches 12 extractors covering 18 extensions
items.summary: (.py, .js/.ts/.tsx via single extractor, .go, .rs, .java, .c/.h, .cpp/.cc/.cxx/.hpp, .rb, .cs, .kt/.kts, .scala, .php). Swift is divergent: detected by detect.py but never extracted because collect_files() glob list AND

items.title: Python is uniquely powerful
items.summary: : it gets the call graph, cross-file uses edges, AND rationale/docstring nodes. The other 11 extractors only emit file/class/function/method/imports/contains. Cross-file uses inference is a two-pass algorithm that fans o

items.title: The semantic subagent prompt is verbatim in skill.md, not in Python.
items.summary: This makes it hot-editable but also non-versioned, non-testable, and not exposed via the MCP server. The prompt defines a 4th knowledge primitive (semantically_similar_to edges + hyperedges) and 6 per-image-type extracti

items.title: The merge logic is also in skill.md, NOT in graphify Python.
items.summary: The graphify package does NOT export a merge_extraction(ast, semantic) function. The "AST nodes win on collision" behavior is implemented in inline Python at skill.md:287-317, NOT in build.py. This is a coupling between

items.title: Evidence labeling is a transport-level guarantee, not a UI convention.
items.summary: Every edge in graph.json carries both confidence (categorical) and confidence_score (numeric, backfilled from _CONFIDENCE_SCORE_DEFAULTS = {EXTRACTED:1.0, INFERRED:0.5, AMBIGUOUS:0.2}). The validator enforces the categor

items.title: Confidence score has TWO sources of truth that produce a subtle inconsistency for INFERRED edges.
items.summary: AST edges never set confidence_score and get the flat default 0.5 from _CONFIDENCE_SCORE_DEFAULTS. Semantic edges emit per-edge scores 0.4–0.9 per the LLM prompt rules. An AST calls edge and a semantic calls edge with th

items.title: Leiden clustering is single-pass second-Leiden split with NO tunable parameters.
items.summary: Hard-coded _MAX_COMMUNITY_FRACTION = 0.25 and _MIN_SPLIT_SIZE = 10. Communities exceeding max(10, int(N * 0.25)) get one re-split attempt; if the second pass returns ≤ 1 community, the original is kept as-is. There is no

items.title: Surprising connections use a 6-factor composite score
items.summary: : confidence weight (AMBIGUOUS=3, INFERRED=2, EXTRACTED=1) + cross file-type bonus (+2 for code↔paper) + cross-repo bonus (+2) + cross-community bonus (+1) + peripheral→hub bonus (+1) + semantic_similarity multiplier (×1

items.title: The PreToolUse hook is a one-line conditional nudge against the exact tools Public uses for raw-file search.
items.summary: Matcher "Glob|Grep", payload [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] && echo '...' || true. Conditional firing (silent when no graph), never blocking (always exits 0), surgical matcher (exactly the raw-search tools). Combined wit

items.title: to_json() is the canonical interchange artifact, and it actively preserves community IDs and edge confidence numerics ra
items.summary: It serializes the NetworkX graph as node-link JSON, injects community onto every node, backfills numeric confidence_score for edges that only have categorical confidence, and carries hyperedges from G.graph["hyperedges"]

items.title: The HTML export is a bounded human-review surface, not a downstream API.
items.summary: to_html() hard-fails above MAX_NODES_FOR_VIZ = 5000 and emits a single self-contained vis.js page with degree-scaled nodes, confidence-styled edges, search, click inspection, community legend filtering, and hyperedge ove

items.title: The Obsidian export is opinionated knowledge packaging, not raw markdown dumping.
items.summary: to_obsidian() writes one note per node with YAML frontmatter, tags by file type / dominant confidence / community, adds [[wikilink]] connections, generates per-community overview notes, embeds a Dataview live query block

items.title: The Canvas export is intentionally lossy: it spatializes communities, rewrites nodes as Obsidian file cards, and caps vi
items.summary: to_canvas() computes a community grid layout, writes group boxes plus per-node file cards, hardcodes note paths under graphify/obsidian/, and keeps only the top 200 weighted edges for readability. Canvas is a presentatio

items.title: Neo4j is supported in two modes: offline Cypher script generation and live driver upserts. Both treat Neo4j as a seconda
items.summary: to_cypher() emits MERGE-based Cypher with node labels derived from file_type and relationships from sanitized relation names, while push_to_neo4j() performs the same mapping directly against a running Neo4j instance. Pub

items.title: The standalone wiki export bakes provenance audit summaries directly into prose articles.
items.summary: to_wiki() writes index.md, one article per community, and one article per supplied god node; each community article lists key concepts, cross-community relationships, source files, and an "Audit Trail" section showing EX

items.title: serve.py makes graph.json the serving boundary by reconstructing communities from node properties instead of depending o
items.summary: The MCP server loads a validated graph path, parses node-link JSON back into NetworkX, and rebuilds the community map purely from each node's serialized community attribute. Any consumer that can read the JSON artifact c

items.title: The MCP serve interface is intentionally narrow: 7 read-only tools, all wrapped as TextContent.
items.summary: The server exposes query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, and shortest_path, and every call returns a single types.TextContent payload rather than structured JSON or typed objects. T

items.title: The serve layer is deliberately thin and mostly static: no cache invalidation, no refresh tool, no watch loop, and no wr
items.summary: The graph is loaded once before tool registration, then the process simply runs the MCP stdio server. Freshness is delegated to the caller rebuilding graph.json out-of-band. REJECT this operational model for Public's pri

items.title: manifest.py is a 4-line re-export shim around detect.py helpers — there is no standalone manifest implementation layer.
items.summary: Whatever "manifest" semantics graphify has are implemented as mtime tracking inside detect.py:237-274. There is no checksum-based, version-stamped, or schema-versioned manifest contract. This means the "manifest" layer i

items.title: build.py is a 42-line module that ONLY validates extraction input and assembles the graph object — no orchestration, no
items.summary: All build orchestration lives in skill.md as inline shell-invoked Python. This confirms K7 (merge logic in skill.md, not Python) and extends it: even the build entrypoint is a thin assembler. Public should ADAPT this onl

items.title: The incremental detection path is mtime-only and has no deletion tombstones.
items.summary: detect_incremental() compares current Path.stat().st_mtime against the saved manifest and partitions files into new_files vs unchanged_files. There is no deleted_files partition: a file removed since the last build silen

items.title: The orchestration layer in skill.md branches the rebuild plan by modality so code-only changes avoid semantic re-extract
items.summary: The code_only branch in skill.md:236-400 runs a fast AST-only re-extract path without invoking the semantic subagent, while changes to documents, papers, or images trigger the full multimodal pipeline. This is the most r

items.title: Cross-corpus check: mixed-corpus worked example does NOT show the multimodal evidence its README claims.
items.summary: karpathy-repos shows non-zero token spend (6,000 input / 3,500 output), 49 files, ~92,616 words, explicit paper and image extraction, and an 81% EXTRACTED / 19% INFERRED mix. The checked-in mixed-corpus/GRAPH_REPORT.md s

items.title: Smaller graphs need stronger inferred-edge skepticism: mixed-corpus shows 50% EXTRACTED / 50% INFERRED versus karpathy-r
items.summary: This is structural: small graphs have fewer high-confidence structural facts, so the relative weight of inferred edges grows. Public's confidence-tier ranking (A1 in §12.1) should account for graph-size-conditional skept

items.title: Relation naming drifts across language extractors: imports_from vs imports.

items.title: Confidence is a one-tier-deep field for non-Python extractors: only EXTRACTED defaults plus INFERRED call edges.

items.summary: (extract.py:2209-2339, 2485-2489). This confirms K5 with line-level evidence and rules out the hypothesis that any non-Python language gets cross-file uses edges. Public should ADOPT the post-pass pattern (file-level imp

items.title: Graphify's validation layer (validate.py) is the most directly portable artifact in the entire repo.
items.summary: It enforces required id, label, type on nodes; required source, target, relation, confidence on edges; bounded confidence ∈ {EXTRACTED, INFERRED, AMBIGUOUS}; and validates hyperedges only structurally. The whole validato

items.title: validate.py schema-boundary validator
items.summary: as a small composable hardening layer for graph/export interchange. Effort: S. Evidence: K32. Why: 71 lines, no dependencies, orthogonal to Code Graph MCP, hardens ingestion boundaries without competing.

items.title: Wiki-style narrative export with EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS audit summaries baked into prose.
items.summary: Effort: S-M. Evidence: K18. Why: distinct from live MCP query surfaces; useful for research-packet handoff and human evidence review.

items.title: Modality-aware rebuild policy layer
items.summary: (code-only fast path vs full multimodal slow path). Effort: M. Evidence: K25. Why: lets Code Graph MCP stay cheap on code-only changes while only invoking CocoIndex/semantic passes when non-code assets actually change.

items.title: Stable JSON interchange artifact preserving community + provenance numerics
items.summary: (the to_json() discipline). Effort: S-M. Evidence: K13, K19. Why: enables stateless serving layers as projections from a durable artifact rather than tightly coupled to the build pipeline.
